likes: The job sequrity, Not much intelligence, technology and skill requirement in job across all 3 departments operation, Electrical and mech.
dislikes:
1. Proper Role Allocation: People working in shifts or maintenance lack proper role allocation. Individuals with a diploma and those with a BE degree perform the same job. Interests in job roles should be considered, but there is currently no system for it.
2. Work-Life Balance: A six-day workweek, rotational shifts, and long commutes to accommodation hinder work-life balance.
3. Better Training: Proper work training for new hires is necessary as they lack experience. Throwing them directly into work makes their tasks significantly unclear, leading to underconfident and stress.
4. Developing Good Work Culture: A good work culture should encourage and motivate each individual, supporting the growth of everyone. Instead of viewing others as competitors, employees should see them as teammates.
5. Proper Hygiene: Hygienic toilet facilities, drinking water, and canteens are missing in some areas.
6. Inter-Divisional Collaboration: Collaboration among all divisions, such as electrical, mecha nical, and operations, is lacking. Instead of addressing problems collectively, divisions avoid issues not related to their own. This behavior increases misunderstandings and quarrels between divisions, which can be avoided through teamwork.
7. Quality Food: Good food is missing.
8. Support from Company and Seniors: There is a lack of care and support from the company, seniors, and the HR team. Employees feel like machines with no support in skill development and learning, making them feel isolated.
9. Unequal Work Distribution: Those who work hard get more work, while those who don't work much spend their day in the canteen or other places without being reprimanded.
10. Value of Women Employees: Women employees are often undervalued and not encouraged to work in technical roles. They are mostly directed towards HR, sales, and planning jobs.
11. It must be incredibly satisfying to watch all those skills, education, and abilities gather dust while you master the art of doing tasks that don't even challenge a fraction of your potential.

likes:
Working in JSW Steel (dolvi) gives these positive experiences to me:
**From GET period, they will give you important role as u become the shift incharge without any training or experience.
**U can consider there is some job securities because company don't fire employees generally.
**Company is targeting to run the plant in automation as much possible.
**There is always buses available for every routes, employees can enjoy this without any price.
dislikes:
There are many things which I dislikes:
**No proper training or learning are given to new GETs/DETs.
**Very dusty and polluted plant, no efforts by company to control or atleast limit the pollution.
**No township is there, specially where the company is giving residency is very far away from plant, it almost take more than 1 hr, simply 8 hr jobs become 11 to 12 hrs.
**Buses always starts late, for ex B shifts end at 10 pm but bus will departure at 11 pm.
**Salary is not upto the mark as the company is making profit or the TATA is giving to their employees.
**Very less manpower is provided, sometimes it becomes hectic to manage all things.
**Canteen' s food is not good also the breakfast (snaks, tea) which is served frequently is not healthy and eatable.
**No job rotation is there, people like me feels boring to do same job. There should be internal job rotation for learning other areas.
**Shift rotation happens for shift people with only one week off, which is not good.
**In nutshell company is more production friendly and less employee friendly.

likes: The only thing I liked about working in jsw Steel is work life balance ( for assistance officer post).
dislikes:
This is really long list
1) salary is very bad , i was working here for 7 years , did you guess how much I was getting?? It's only 25k per month for 7 years of experience only bachelors can survive with this salary. I had some friends who were married , and saw them struggling to meet their day-to-day expenses.
2) About increments: average increments ranging from 4% for average performance to 12% to outstanding.Even if you get outstanding, below L8 grade get peanut because of low basic salary.(40% of salary consists of variable incentive which depends upon achieving targets) and increment percentage is same for all grades.
3) About company policy: A worst company policy which deals with the accidents/fatal happening inside of plant, according to this policy if a person dies inside the plant during his working hours then the variable incentive of the department which he belongs becomes zero.(Think about those employees who took any loan). 
4) About work culture: L8 and above grades are management level grades, and they are considered as Angels came straight from heaven. Priority were given for them for everything (obviously only for good things).

likes: As working part the company is very good.You will get the good opportunity to learn new things. Job security 100%.No one threw you out unless you have have some really serious things. But when it comes to the personnel growth like promotion etc you will have to wait, company policy 4 years minimum to get a promotion and after 4 years you have to depend on the relationship with your seniors not how you work, how you are with your seniors it's reflect on your increment. Work life balance is zero. If you are planning for the long term in this company with your family life forget it.. You are not able to get that in terms of family. Trainings are only on the papers, work pressure is too much. There are others plant of JSW in many location of India but you not able to your transfer. Only in some cases I have seen people getting transfer but after running for many months to different people. As per the salery what is on the paper(salery slip)deduct 15000 and the remaining amount you will get as a in hand salery. For the quarters, if u have joined in dy. Manager and above u ll get the quarters  but below that it really a mess for getting quarters. Travel from there especially to the north India is quite hectic.
dislikes: Politics is the major issue. Within the department. Within the teams. It quite  difficult to get cross it.

likes: 1)Job Security is Good, but in today's scenario it is just fair.
dislikes:
1)Work Life balance is totally Zero,They treat employees as waged labours. 
2)The normal working hr. as per paper is from 9-30 to 5-30 but hardly any day you can punch out at 5:30pm. They force you to work till 7:30 and if you are working at remote locations like Pen(near to Alibagh) add 2 more hours for travelling.My current scenario is I catch morning bus @7:15 and return at home not before 9:30 pm.
Day-by-day the HRs are nullifying the employee benefits to bake their own bread at the cost of lower employees life.
3)When you will see company's profit sheet, you can easily figure out that this is the company with least employee expenses as compared to their counterpart.
They consider employee's salary as one of their mode of making money and they can deduct any amount from your salary. Example- Production Incentive, which is always lesser than what offered.
One more strange thing, they say it bonus but they deduct from your CTC and provide the same x amt at the end of Year.
